postData.contentHtml:
Day two of The Oval test saw England battling hard against New Zealand, with young opener Emilio Gay emerging as a beacon of stability for the home side. After a costly run-out, England found themselves pegged back, but Gay stood firm, reaching a vital 48 runs by tea. His composed innings helped England reach 222-6 in response to New Zealand's formidable first-innings total of 391.

Gay's performance is particularly significant as he's been given a golden opportunity to cement his place at the top of England's batting order, a position previously held by Zak Crawley. His inclusion in the squad, alongside James Rew and Ollie Robinson, highlights a new-look England side.

The cricketing world has reacted with a largely positive sentiment on social media, acknowledging Gay's crucial contribution under pressure, though some debate around the match's broader context also features in discussions. With a challenging deficit to overcome, Gay's resilience provides a much-needed foundation for England's fightback in this intriguing test match.
postData.title: Gay's Gritty Knock: Steadying England at The Oval
postData.summary: Emilio Gay shines with a crucial 48 runs, helping England stabilize their innings against New Zealand on Day 2 of The Oval Test after a tricky start.

sources.title: Gay steadies England after a costly run out and is 48 at tea at the Oval against New Zealand
Gay, Rew, Robinson named for England's first test against New Zealand
